description: Karpathy LLM Wiki integration — adopt the three-layer (raw / wiki / schema) pattern with three skills (/wiki-ingest, /wiki-query, /wiki-lint) plus context/rules/wiki.md as the schema doc. Locked by /interview, audited by pm-agent ultrathink, gated by 2 critics; all HIGH findings mitigated in prd.md ACs.

userStories.title: Scaffold wiki/ + context/rules/wiki.md schema rule + wiki/README.md template
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a tracked wiki/ directory with a complete schema rule and an empty index template so subsequent skills have a known surface to write into.
acceptanceCriteria: wiki/ exists at repo root, tracked, with wiki/README.md per context/rules/directory-readme.md (one-line intent + subfolder enumeration + pointer to context/rules/wiki.md)
wiki/raw/ exists with wiki/raw/README.md (one-line intent describing immutable WebFetch snapshots and sources: provenance)

.gitignore appended with EXACTLY two lines at end-of-file: `wiki/raw/*` then `!wiki/raw/README.md` — verified via `git check-ignore -v wiki/raw/README.md` (exit 1) and `git check-ignore -v wiki/raw/2026-05-24-test.md` (exit 0)

context/rules/wiki.md ships COMPLETE with all 7 required sections (each non-empty): (1) Boundary table — verbatim 7-row table from plan; (2) Entry schema with full frontmatter spec + H1 + ## Summary + ## Detail + ## See Also + ≤600-word cap + sub-article split; (3) Slug derivation rule: URL path's last non-UUID segment, lowercased kebab-case, charset [a-z0-9-]+; --slug override for UUID URLs; (4) Cross-link convention `[[slug]]` with grep pattern `grep -roE '\[\[[a-z0-9-]+\]\]' wiki/`; (5) Confidence lifecycle: provisional set by /wiki-ingest, confirmed/deprecated set MANUALLY by orchestrator only; /wiki-lint REPORTS deprecated but never AUTONOMOUSLY SETS; (6) Frontmatter extraction canonical command: `awk '/^---$/{f=!f; next} f{print}' wiki/<slug>.md` — locked for both /wiki-query and /wiki-lint; (7) Body-merge strategy for /wiki-ingest updates: replace ## Summary + ## Detail in-place, append to sources: + See Also, update updated:, never touch created:
Schema example in context/rules/wiki.md either drops memory/MEMORY.md#<anchor> from sources: OR annotates as aspirational (MEMORY.md has no stable anchors)

wiki/README.md ships with empty entries-table header only: exact header line `| Slug | Title | Tags | Updated |` + alignment row `| --- | --- | --- | --- |`; sorted by updated: descending; empty corpus = header lines only (not an error)
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] ### Added entry in same commit: 'wiki/ directory + context/rules/wiki.md schema for Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern'
git status shows only the expected new files (no leakage into memory/MEMORY.md or other surfaces)
Typecheck passes (satisfied by clean git status on this AC scope)
userStories.notes: Wave 1. No deps. Model recommendation: Sonnet (Haiku risks shipping a stub satisfying section names but failing the 'each non-empty' requirement).

userStories.title: Implement /wiki-ingest skill (URL + path + --from-draft + update-existing)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a skill that reads a source URL/path/draft, snapshots it to wiki/raw/, and writes or updates entity pages in wiki/.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/wiki-ingest/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter: name: wiki-ingest, description, argument-hint: '<url|path> [--slug <override>] | --from-draft <slug> [--allow-stale]'
Argument interface locked: /wiki-ingest <url|path> [--slug <override>] for source ingest; /wiki-ingest --from-draft <slug> [--allow-stale] for draft promotion; no other invocation forms
Skill `mkdir -p wiki/raw/` before writing any snapshot (directory is gitignored; may not exist on fresh clone)
Slug derivation follows context/rules/wiki.md § Slug derivation rule (US-001); for UUID/hash URLs, --slug <override> required and skill errors if absent
URL ingest: WebFetch the URL, write snapshot to wiki/raw/<yyyy-mm-dd>-<slug>.md with header `# Source: <url>` then fetched body; date = `date -u +%Y-%m-%d`
Path ingest: read file, write copy to wiki/raw/<yyyy-mm-dd>-<basename>.md; same UTC date convention

--from-draft resolution: glob `memory/*/wiki-drafts/<slug>.md`, sort by ISO date in parent directory name (lexicographic max), exclude `.skip` files; if most-recent directory date > 7 days older than today UTC, exit STALE unless --allow-stale; do NOT use filesystem mtime

Write/update wiki/<slug>.md with valid frontmatter per context/rules/wiki.md schema: title, slug, tags, created (only on create), updated (always), sources (appending new snapshot path), related (optional), confidence: provisional (on create)

Update path IN SCOPE: when wiki/<slug>.md exists, follow context/rules/wiki.md § Body-merge strategy (replace ## Summary + ## Detail in-place, append to sources: + See Also, update updated:, never touch created:); do NOT concatenate bodies
Daily log entry per context/rules/memory.md MIP: `## /wiki-ingest -- HH:MM UTC` with fields Source, Slug-Created, Slugs-Updated, Snapshot-Path, Result, Observation
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] ### Added entry in same commit: '/wiki-ingest skill for capturing WebFetch snapshots and authoring wiki entries'

Manual QA smoke test (NOT automated; CI uses local fixture if testing): `/wiki-ingest https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f --slug karpathy-llm-wiki` creates wiki/raw/<today>-karpathy-llm-wiki.md AND wiki/karpathy-llm-wiki.md with valid frontmatter and confidence: provisional; this smoke MUST commit BEFORE US-003 smoke runs
Typecheck passes (no project type-check; satisfied by clean git diff + SKILL.md frontmatter parses as valid YAML)
userStories.notes: Wave 2. Depends on US-001. Can run in parallel with US-003 (same wave). Model: Sonnet. NOTE: smoke test must commit before US-003 smoke runs — /delegate wave planner must enforce this ordering.

userStories.title: Implement /wiki-query skill (frontmatter grep + top-3 read cap)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a skill that searches the wiki by topic and reads the top match(es) into context.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/wiki-query/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter: name: wiki-query, description, argument-hint: '<topic>'; SKILL.md body documents multi-word semantics: split on whitespace into space-separated OR terms, union of per-term matches deduplicated

Greps only the frontmatter of each wiki/*.md entry using the canonical extraction command from context/rules/wiki.md § Frontmatter extraction canonical command: `awk '/^---$/{f=!f; next} f{print}' wiki/<slug>.md` — same command used by /wiki-lint (US-004)
Matches against frontmatter title, slug, and tags only (NOT body text)
Returns list of all matching wiki/*.md file paths to stdout
Empty-result behavior: Match-Count = 0 prints `No wiki entries matched <topic>` and exits status 0 (NOT an error)
Sub-cap behavior: 0 < Match-Count < 3 reads ALL matches into context; Match-Count >= 3 reads top 3 only; cap hardcoded to 3 in v1 (raising requires SKILL.md edit)
Tie-breaking order: updated: descending (most recently updated first)
Reads individual wiki/*.md files directly (does NOT route via wiki/README.md index)
Daily log entry: `## /wiki-query -- HH:MM UTC` with fields Query, Match-Count, Read-Slugs (or — if zero), Result, Observation
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] ### Added entry in same commit: '/wiki-query skill for grep-based wiki search with top-3 read cap'

Manual QA smoke test (after US-002 smoke commits): `/wiki-query karpathy` returns wiki/karpathy-llm-wiki.md path and reads it into context; `/wiki-query nonexistent-topic-xyz` prints empty-result message and exits 0
Typecheck passes (satisfied by clean git diff + SKILL.md frontmatter parses as valid YAML)
userStories.notes: Wave 2 (parallel with US-002). Depends on US-001. Smoke test runs AFTER US-002 smoke commits. Model: Sonnet.

userStories.title: Implement /wiki-lint skill (stale + orphan + broken-link + atomic README regen)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a manual health-check skill that surfaces stale/orphaned/broken entries and regenerates the wiki/README.md index.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/skills/wiki-lint/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter: name: wiki-lint, description, argument-hint: '[--dry-run]'
Default invocation regenerates wiki/README.md entries table; --dry-run prints what would change without writing

Atomic regeneration: write to wiki/README.md.tmp; validate non-empty AND contains exact header `| Slug | Title | Tags | Updated |`; atomically rename on success; on failure exit non-zero, leave original intact, print failure reason
Stale check (a): enumerate entries with frontmatter updated: > 90 days older than today UTC; reported as separate finding type with 'consider review' recommendation

Stale check (b): enumerate entries with frontmatter confidence: deprecated; reported as separate finding type with 'consider archive or delete' recommendation; (a) and (b) NEVER conflated; /wiki-lint REPORTS deprecated entries but NEVER SETS the flag autonomously

Orphan check: two-pass; pass 1 enumerates all slugs from wiki/*.md frontmatter using same canonical extraction as US-003; pass 2 greps all entry bodies for `[[slug]]` (pattern `\[\[[a-z0-9-]+\]\]`); entries with zero inbound refs are orphans; single-entry corpus orphan is TRUE POSITIVE (not false positive)
Broken-outbound-link check: grep entry bodies for `[[slug]]` where slug does not match any existing entry slug; reported separately from orphans

Contradiction detection: descoped for v1; stub function prints `contradiction detection: not yet implemented — see wiki-lint follow-up tracking` (no #N placeholder; follow-up tracking issue may be filed post-merge but is NOT a hard blocker)

README regeneration format: entries table `| Slug | Title | Tags | Updated |` (literal header in that order), alignment row `| --- | --- | --- | --- |`, sorted by updated: descending; empty corpus = header lines only (NOT an error)
Daily log entry: `## /wiki-lint -- HH:MM UTC` with fields Entries-Scanned, Stale-90d, Deprecated, Orphaned, Broken-Links, Mode (op/dry-run), Result, Observation
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] ### Added entry in same commit: '/wiki-lint skill for manual wiki health checks + index regeneration'

Manual QA smoke test (one-entry corpus post US-002): `/wiki-lint --dry-run` reports zero stale-90d AND zero deprecated findings (single-entry orphan is a TRUE POSITIVE — smoke test 'zero false positives' refers only to stale + deprecated + broken-link on the fresh entry); set entry confidence: deprecated and rerun, deprecated check surfaces it; without --dry-run regenerates wiki/README.md to include the entry under header lines
Typecheck passes (satisfied by clean git diff + SKILL.md frontmatter parses as valid YAML)
userStories.notes: Wave 3. Depends on US-001, US-002, US-003 (needs canonical extraction + at least one ingested entry for smoke). Model: Sonnet.

userStories.title: Register /wiki-ingest, /wiki-query, /wiki-lint in AGENTS.md skills table
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want the three new wiki skills registered in the AGENTS.md skills table so they appear in the auto-loaded orchestrator context.
acceptanceCriteria: DEPENDENCY GATE: DO NOT execute until US-002, US-003, US-004 commits are landed (their skill names and descriptions must exist before this story can author authoritative AGENTS.md rows)

Symlink integrity check (CRITICAL): `ls -li CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md` shows the same inode BEFORE editing; edit ONLY AGENTS.md (Edit tool refuses to write through CLAUDE.md symlink); after editing rerun `ls -li CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md` and verify inode still matches
AGENTS.md skills table receives 3 new rows appended at the bottom of the existing table — NO new category header (table convention is flat)

Each row format matches adjacent rows: `| /wiki-ingest | When capturing a source (URL, path) or promoting a sub-agent draft to a wiki entry |` (one-line 'When' guidance; references the locked argument interfaces from US-002/3/4)
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] receives one additional ### Changed line in same commit: 'Register /wiki-ingest, /wiki-query, /wiki-lint in AGENTS.md skills table'
No duplicate CHANGELOG entries: verify with `grep -c 'wiki-ingest' CHANGELOG.md` returning the expected count (1 for US-002's Added entry + 1 for US-005's Changed entry = 2) before commit
Typecheck passes (satisfied by clean git diff)
userStories.notes: Wave 4 (serial; not parallelizable). Depends on US-002, US-003, US-004 all committing first. Model: Haiku.
